title: Ultrasound probe for cardiac treatment
abstract: Ultrasound probe systems and methods for using an ultrasound probe to assist during treatment of conditions of the human heart are provided. The method may comprise loading a medical instrument into a guide fastened to the ultrasound probe. The ultrasound probe may be inserted into a patient by way of an incision. The distal end of the ultrasound probe may be navigated to a location adjacent to an exterior surface of the heart. A treatment site may be identified on the exterior surface of the heart based on images obtained from an ultrasound transducer disposed within a distal end of the ultrasound probe. The medical instrument may be advanced within the guide to the treatment site and the medical instrument used for treatment. After treatment, the medical instrument may be withdrawn from the treatment site.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for treatment of a heart, the method comprising: loading a medical instrument into a guide attached to an ultrasound probe, the ultrasound probe comprising an elongate shaft extending from a proximal handle to a distal end of the ultrasound probe; inserting the ultrasound probe into a patient by way of an opening in skin of the patient; navigating the distal end of the ultrasound probe to a location adjacent to an exterior surface of the heart or pericardium; identifying a treatment site on the exterior surface using an image obtained from an ultrasound transducer disposed within the distal end of the ultrasound probe; advancing the medical instrument to the treatment site using the guide; using the medical instrument at the treatment site; and withdrawing the medical instrument away from the treatment site. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising attaching the guide to the ultrasound probe by way of a proximal coupling and a distal coupling such that the guide is angled at a fixed angle with respect to the elongate shaft and a distal end of the medical instrument is adjacent to the distal end of the ultrasound probe. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein inserting the ultrasound probe comprises advancing the distal end of the ultrasound probe and the distal end of the medical instrument together within the patient. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein navigating the distal end of the ultrasound probe to the location comprises viewing tissues and structures within the patient by way of the ultrasound transducer, such that injury to the tissues and 

 structures due to advancing the distal end of the ultrasound probe and the medical instrument are minimized. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the ultrasound transducer is configured to provide a view of tissues and structures directly in front of the distal end of the ultrasound probe during navigating the distal end of the ultrasound probe to the location. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, further comprising treating a condition of the heart by fixating a splint within the heart using at least one anchor so as to alter a geometric shape of the heart. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the condition of the heart is mitral valve incompetency and the splint is a mitral valve splint, and wherein treating the condition of the heart further comprises treating the mitral valve incompetency by fixating the mitral valve splint within the heart using the at least one anchor. 8. The method of claim 6 or 7, wherein the at least one anchor comprises a self- expandable anchor suitable for deployment from a lumen of a delivery catheter, wherein the self-expandable anchor is configured to contact the exterior surface of the heart or pericardium adjacent a left ventricle of the heart. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the medical instrument comprises a flexible needle, and wherein identifying a treatment site comprises identifying a first puncture site on the exterior surface of the heart or pericardium based on observing structures on the exterior surface and within the heart using the ultrasound transducer. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein using the medical instrument at the treatment site comprises using the flexible needle to puncture the exterior surface at the first puncture site. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising identifying a second puncture site on a septal wall of the heart using the ultrasound transducer, and wherein using the medical instrument at the treatment site comprises passing the flexible needle through the septal wall into the left ventricle of the heart at the second puncture site. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising identifying a third puncture site on a posterior wall of the left ventricle of the heart using the ultrasound transducer, and wherein using the medical instrument at the treatment site further comprises puncturing the posterior wall at the third puncture site. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the flexible needle includes a plurality of slits along a length thereof, and wherein puncturing the posterior wall comprises extending an inner curved needle from the flexible needle through the posterior wall at the third puncture site. 14. The method of claim 12 or 13, wherein using the medical instrument at the treatment site further comprises deploying an anchor through the flexible needle and placing the anchor in contact with an exterior surface of the heart or pericardium adjacent the left ventricle of the heart. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the ultrasound probe is a transvaginal ultrasound probe, and wherein inserting the ultrasound probe into the patient comprises inserting the trans-vaginal ultrasound probe into the patient. 16. A method for treatment of the human heart, the method comprising: loading a medical instrument into a guide attached to a trans-vaginal ultrasound probe, the trans-vaginal ultrasound probe comprising an elongate shaft extending from a proximal handle to a distal end; inserting the trans-vaginal ultrasound probe into a patient by way of an opening in skin of the patient; 

 navigating the distal end of the trans-vaginal ultrasound probe to a location adjacent to an exterior surface of the heart or pericardium; identifying a first treatment site on the exterior surface based on data about the exterior surface obtained using an ultrasound transducer disposed within the distal end of the trans -vaginal ultrasound probe; advancing the medical instrument to the first treatment site using the guide; using the medical instrument at the first treatment site; identifying a second treatment site on an interior wall of the heart based on data about the interior wall obtained using the ultrasound transducer; advancing the medical instrument to the second treatment site; using the medical instrument at the second treatment site; and withdrawing the medical instrument from the heart. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the medical instrument comprises a flexible needle, and wherein identifying a first treatment site comprises identifying a first puncture site on the exterior surface of the heart or pericardium based on observing structures on the exterior surface and within the heart using the ultrasound transducer. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein using the medical instrument at the first treatment site comprises using the flexible needle to puncture the exterior surface of the heart or pericardium at the first puncture site, and wherein using the medical instrument at the second treatment site comprises passing the flexible needle through a wall of a septum of the heart and into a left ventricle of the heart. 19. An anchoring system for treatment of functional mitral valve regurgitation, comprising: a delivery catheter including a lumen; an expandable anchor for fixating a mitral valve splint, the anchor having a low profile configuration in which it may be positioned inside the lumen of the delivery catheter and an expanded configuration 

 configured to anchor the mitral valve splint, wherein the expandable anchor is configured to transition to the expanded configuration outside the delivery catheter; and an ultrasound probe system including an ultrasound probe and a guide, the guide capable of guiding the delivery catheter to a desired location, wherein the ultrasound probe is configured to allow a user to view the expandable anchor and tissue of the heart during deployment of the expandable anchor inside a patient. The anchoring system of claim 19, wherein the expandable anchor is a self- expandable anchor, and wherein the self-expandable anchor comprises a wire having atraumatic ends which meet at a break, the wire capable of being straightened from a ring-shaped configuration in the expanded configuration to a straightened configuration to transition the anchor into the low profile configuration, the self-expandable anchor also comprising a cover supported by the wire, and wherein the cover is engaged with a tension member such that pulling the tension member tightens the cover into a cone-like configuration.
